rationale: Final 5-book W40K wave closes the loop at 565/565 (cumulativeBefore=560). Three Black Library novellas frame tight single-faction character pieces (Custodes hunt on Vorganthian, Necron court at Doahht, Imperial Guard breakout from an ork camp), and two full novels bookend the Imperium's edges — Alpha Legion empire-building under Solomon Akurra and Cypher's unreliable raid on the Throneworld during the Great Rift. Coverage solid across all five, including freshly-rated Ghost Legion (Feb 2026). | Extended 2026-06-10 (claude-opus-4-8[1m]): appended W40K-0566..0570, the first five books of the weekly-refresh promotion wave (Brief 133). They fall inside this domain-end restbatch's positional slot, so loop:next never surfaced them on its own; adding them fills 057 to a full 10-book batch. New five: Hive (Abnett hive-noir), Da Freebooterz Code (Ork pirates), World Ablaze (Armageddon anthology), Legends of the Waaagh! (Ork omnibus), Veterans of the Fall (Cadian-survivors novella). Hive and World Ablaze are 2026 titles with no Goodreads edition yet (unrated).

overrides.synopsis: Solomon Akurra, the Alpha Legion Harrowmaster known as the Ghost, has united warring renegade factions under his banner and conquered entire star systems. Wielding what he claims is the Pale Spear — the lost weapon of his primarch — Solomon dreams not of plunder but of empire: a future in which the Imperium is shattered and humanity rebuilt in his own image. As his burgeoning new world draws the gaze of the Imperium, Officio Assassinorum killers begin to circle in the shadows and fury festers within his own ranks. Forced to play the long game against enemies without and dissent within, the Ghost must decide what price he is willing to pay for a vision that may yet consume him and the Legion he has only just begun to forge.

As the Great Rift tears open the sky above Terra and daemons walk upon the Throneworld for the first time in ten thousand years, Cypher and a small band of the Fallen escape from the most secure prison in the Imperium. Loose within the Imperial Palace itself, they are hunted by warriors of the Dark Angels, the golden Custodes of the Adeptus Custodes and the killers of the Officio Assassinorum — yet Cypher's true agenda runs far beyond mere escape. He intends to stand before the Emperor on the Golden Throne, though whether he comes to bow, to plead or to murder his god, even his closest companions cannot say. Told through Cypher's own unreliable voice, this is a tale of secrets, lies and a war waged at the heart of mankind.

When Shield-Captain Cartovandis receives a mysterious distress call from the world of Vorganthian, out on the fringe of the Sol system, he gathers a small band of Custodians to investigate. They arrive to find a planet in the grip of self-annihilation, its cities overrun and its peacekeepers vanished or worse. Meanwhile on Terra, Captain-General Trajann Valoris dispatches a second hand-picked trio of his order on the trail of a dangerous artefact stolen from deep beneath the Imperial Palace itself. As the two missions slowly converge, the Custodes must hunt down the conspirators behind Vorganthian's fall before the madness consuming that world reaches its true target — something far closer to the Throne than any of them had ever feared.

flags.reason: Chaos antagonist on Vorganthian described as 'cultists' in available coverage; no specific Chaos sub-faction named, so the cult is left unbound rather than mis-tagged.

overrides.synopsis: Deep in the Ghoul Stars, a rebellion threatens the long, deathless reign of Nemesor Zahndrekh and his ever-watchful bodyguard, Vargard Obyron. Tasked by the Phaeron of the Sautekh Dynasty to put the uprising down, the two ancient necrons join forces with an old acquaintance, the Nemesor Setekh, and set course for the world of Doahht — a place where the slumbering legions never woke properly, and the dead now stagger as little more than empty shells. As Zahndrekh, still convinced his court is one of flesh and blood, dreams his stately way through the campaign, Obyron alone must reckon with the horror beneath the broken world, the ghosts of his own ages-long service, and what Setekh truly wants from the dark.

Veteran Guardsman Marcus van Veenan thought he had found a quiet posting to see out the last of his years in the Astra Militarum — until the world of Rotauri fell beneath Warboss 'Eadbasha's gathering Waaagh!. Taken alive together with several hundred of his comrades, van Veenan is dragged into the heart of the orks' nightmarish stronghold and put to work building a colossal idol to their savage gods. With Imperial discipline crumbling around him and the fate of his regiment resting on shoulders far older than they once were, the weary sergeant must rally the prisoners for a desperate breakout — not only to escape the camp alive, but to carry warning back to the Imperium of the storm now gathering across the sector.

Sacramentus is a hive city on the edge. The planetary governor has fallen amid scandal and treachery, and the Imperial authorities have installed an outsider to fill the void — an unpopular choice in a rough, dogmatic city where austerity bites deep and Adeptus Arbites patrols enforce the Lex Imperialis without mercy or sympathy. In the upper spires, whispers of rebellion grow louder. In the depths, rival gangs carve out territory and forbidden cults grow bolder, feeding on resentment and desperation. But the visible unrest is not the greatest threat to Sacramentus. Something darker stirs in the lower reaches — something that the Arbites sense but cannot yet name, and that no faction scheming for control of the hive is prepared for.

flags.reason: A hidden Chaos cult is the antagonist, but no specific patron god or sub-faction is confirmed in pre-release coverage, so it is left unbound rather than guessed.
No named POV characters are confirmed in pre-release sources; the novel uses multiple shifting perspectives.

overrides.synopsis: Skeeg Horntoof has one dream: the biggest hat, the most dakka, and a name that echoes across the void. As kaptin of Gork's Revenge, he commands his crew of Freebooterz by Da Freebooterz Code — a set of rules so outrageous they just might work. His latest plan is his boldest yet: plunder the hunting grounds of Lord Captain Antoinette von Hume, the merciless pirate queen who has terrorised the Halo Stars for decades. But von Hume did not earn her legend by suffering upstart Ork kaptins gladly, and she has no intention of letting Horntoof walk away with her prize. A raucous clash of pirates — green and otherwise — in the lawless dark beyond the Imperium's reach.

Eleven stories. One burning world. World Ablaze gathers an ensemble of Black Library voices to chronicle the relentless defence of Armageddon — a war world where every hive block, ash waste, and frozen wasteland has become a killing ground. Blood Angels fight desperate rearguard actions, Black Templars drag their wounded across miles of ice, Space Wolves and Salamanders forge an unlikely brotherhood in the crucible, and Crimson Fists make their final stand against the greenskin tide. Astra Militarum troopers find salvation in the most unlikely places, while veteran ork pilots pursue one last moment of glory. Across every story, the scale of the invasion bears down on soldiers great and ordinary alike, showing the war for Armageddon as it has never been told before.

Three of Black Library's finest authors — Dan Abnett, Aaron Dembski-Bowden, and Sandy Mitchell — deliver two novels, a novella, and three short stories united by one green tide. In Helsreach, the Black Templars make a ferocious last stand to defend Hive Helsreach on Armageddon as an ork horde of unprecedented scale crashes over the city walls. In Caves of Ice, Commissar Ciaphas Cain leads Valhallan Ice Warriors on a frozen mining world where an ork invasion masks something far older and more dangerous stirring beneath the permafrost. In I Am Slaughter, the Imperial Fists depart Terra for the first time in centuries only to find a xenos onslaught that threatens the entire Imperium. Three short stories round out the collection, each placing soldiers, commissars, and Space Marines face-to-face with the unrelenting ferocity of the Waaagh.

flags.reason: No named ork warbosses are confirmed for this omnibus; Orks appear as the collective antagonist across the collected stories.

overrides.synopsis: When Cadia fell, a handful of traumatised survivors found themselves stranded on a ruined world carved apart by rival cults. Since then, the Kasrkin veterans known as the Tattershields have anchored the crumbling Imperial defence, holding back the overwhelming might of the Archenemy through sabotage, assassinations, and unbreakable discipline. Their commander, Sergeant Kostantin Taikon, is an inspiration to the beleaguered defenders — but he is haunted by guilt and consumed by fatalism, fighting on with no hope of victory or atonement. As infiltrators sow discord in their ranks and a sadistic new enemy closes in, Taikon must lead his indomitable Kasrkin to the bitter end. Collecting the short story Tattershield, the serial Tatters of Hope, and the novella Veterans of the Fall.

flags.reason: The antagonist is named only as 'the Archenemy' and 'rival cults'; no specific Chaos warband or Legion is identified in available sources, so it is left unbound.
